title: Spot the Silent Change (Synonymous SNP)
learning_goal: Learn that a Single Nucleotide Polymorphism (SNP) can change a codon's letters without changing the amino acid it codes for, because the genetic code is redundant (several codons can encode the same amino acid). This is called a synonymous (silent) mutation.
rules: You'll work with whichever gene you chose in Level 1 - your choice carries over automatically.
Both the reference and your sample DNA sequences are shown in full right away - your job is to spot the ONE nucleotide that differs between them.
Click directly on the nucleotide in the sample row that you think differs from the reference.
Once you find it, the affected codon's amino acid is blanked out - use the codon table to work out what it should be and fill it in yourself.
A single nucleotide change does not always change the amino acid - the protein comparison at the end tells you whether it did here.
materials_provided: The reference (known/healthy) nucleotide sequence and full amino acid sequence, shown on screen the whole time
Your sample's full nucleotide sequence, shown alongside the reference so you can click to locate the ONE nucleotide difference
An amino acid picker (all 20 amino acids plus the '*' STOP symbol) to fill in the blanked codon once you've found the SNP
instructions: 1. Compare the reference DNA row against your sample DNA row, letter by letter, and click the ONE nucleotide in the sample that differs.
2. The codon containing that nucleotide is revealed as a blank in your protein row. Look up the changed codon in the codon table and click the amino acid you think it now codes for.
3. Once correct, compare your completed protein row against the reference protein row. Are they the same or different at the position of the SNP?
4. If the amino acid is unchanged, the game confirms: 'Synonymous mutation - the protein is unaffected!' and explains why using the codon table (both codons map to the same letter).

mutation_details.change: T -> C at the 3rd (wobble) position of codon 9
mutation_details.classification: Synonymous (silent) SNP - both ATT and ATC code for Isoleucine, so the protein sequence does not change.

mutation_details.change: T -> C at the 3rd (wobble) position of codon 9
mutation_details.classification: Synonymous (silent) SNP - both GTT and GTC code for Valine, so the protein sequence does not change.

mutation_details.change: A -> G at the 3rd (wobble) position of codon 9
mutation_details.classification: Synonymous (silent) SNP - both AAA and AAG code for Lysine, so the protein sequence does not change.
